perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care; not use the Confidential Information, or permit it to be accessed or used, for any purpose other than the Purpose, including without limitation, to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same. Recipient will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care, unless disclosure is required by a court order; not use the Confidential Information, or permit it to be accessed or used, for any purpose other than the Purpose, including without limitation, to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same. Recipient will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: Section 3 is modified to include an exception to the confidentiality obligation: 'unless disclosure is required by a court order.' This addition creates a contradiction with Section 4, which already details the procedure for Required Disclosure, including providing notice and assistance to the Disclosing Party. This change creates confusion: Section 3 implies that the recipient could disclose without adhering to what is stated in Section 4, if a court order demands it. The addition of the clause 'unless disclosure is required by a court order' provides an alternative which diminishes the purpose of what's specified in Section 4. The uncertainty arises around whether adhering to Section 4's procedure is optional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any Disclosure by the Recipient or its Represent atives of any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information pursuant to applicable federal, state or local law, regulation or a valid order issued by a court or governmental agency of competent jurisdiction (a " Legal Order ") shall be subject to the te rms of this Section. Prior to making any such disclosure, the Recipient shall make commercially reasonable efforts to provide the Disclosing Party with: (a) prompt written notice of such requirement so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other remedy; and (b) reasonable assistance in opposing such disclosure or seeking a protective order or other limitations on disclosure. If, after providing such notice and assistance as required herein, the Recipient remains subject to a Legal Order to disclose any Confidential Information, the Recipient (or its Representatives or other persons to whom such Legal Order is directed) shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which, on the advice of the Recipient's legal counsel, such Legal Order specifically requires .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation ('unless disclosure is required by a court order') that directly contradicts the procedure outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' (Section 4) for handling disclosures required by legal orders. Section 4 specifies a process of notice and assistance to the Disclosing Party before any such disclosure. The added exception in the Changed Text implies that the Recipient might be able to disclose without adhering to Section 4's procedure, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about the applicability of Section 4.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or at the Disclosing Party's request at any time during the term of this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives shall promptly return to the Disclosing Party all copies, whether in written, electronic or other form or media, of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, or destroy all such copies and confirm the same in writing to the Disclosing Party ; provid ed, that the Recipient and its Representatives may retain such Confidential Information as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or at the Disclosing Party's request at any time during the term of this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives shall promptly return to the Disclosing Party all copies, whether in written, electronic or other form or media, of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, or destroy all such copies and confirm the same in writing to the Disclosing Party ; provid ed, that the Recipient and its Representatives may retain such Confidential Information as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies or for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 5, adding 'or for archival purposes' to the permitted reasons for retaining Confidential Information introduces ambiguity. This conflicts with Section 6, which sets a specific 'Confidential Period' after which obligations survive, even after return or destruction of information. The addition of the exception allows the Recipient to keep information indefinitely. The original clause clearly states obligations will survive after the destruction of the data, but the new exception introduces uncertainty, and contradicts the confidential period specified on Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effect ive Date and shall expire 18 months from the Effective Date, provided that either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time by providing written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, each Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement , irrespective of termination of this Agreement , shall survive until the 18 month anniversary of this Agreement , even after the return or destruction of Confidential Information by the Recipient (the " Confidential Period "), provid ed that for any and all trade secrets of the Disclosing Party, the Confidential Period shall last for as long as such Confidential Information qualifies as a trade secret under applicable federal, state and/or local law.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Recipient to retain confidential information only as necessary to comply with document retention policies. The changed text adds 'or for archival purposes', which allows the Recipient to keep the information indefinitely. This directly contradicts the 'Confidential Period' specified in the contradicted text (Section 6), which requires all confidential information, even trade secrets, to be protected for a defined duration, irrespective of termination of this Agreement. The added exception introduces ambiguity and undermines the enforceability of the Confidential Period. This is a clear contradiction as the added clause introduces ambiguity where certainty is expected, adhering to the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, the Recipi ent and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Disclosing Party for the purpose of hiring them, solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Disclosing Party or outside of the ordinary course of business, dir ectly or indirectly contact or participate in communications with any disclosed companies, entities or persons (including each of thei r affiliates, parents or subsidiaries). Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Recipient and its Representatives shall not be restricted from hiring any employee of Disclosing Party who responds to a general solicitation for employment not directed towards the Disclosing Party’s employees.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, the Recipi ent and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Disclosing Party for the purpose of hiring them, solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Disclosing Party or outside of the ordinary course of business, dir ectly or indirectly contact or participate in communications with any disclosed companies, entities or persons (including each of thei r affiliates, parents or subsidiaries). However, the Recipient and its Representatives shall be permitted to hire employees of the disclosing party as long as they wait at least 6 months after the employee is no longer working at the disclosing party. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Recipient and its Representatives shall not be restricted from hiring any employee of Disclosing Party who responds to a general solicitation for employment not directed towards the Disclosing Party’s employees.
perturbation.explanation: The addition that 'the Recipient and its Representatives shall be permitted to hire employees of the disclosing party as long as they wait at least 6 months after the employee is no longer working at the disclosing party.' creates a contradiction with the opening statement of Section 10 ('During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, the Recipi ent and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Disclosing Party for the purpose of hiring them'). This contradictory statement undermines the purpose of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, the Recipi ent and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Disclosing Party for the purpose of hiring them, solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Disclosing Party or outside of the ordinary course of business, dir ectly or indirectly contact or participate in communications with any disclosed companies, entities or persons (including each of thei r affiliates, parents or subsidiaries). However, the Recipient and its Representatives shall be permitted to hire employees of the disclosing party as long as they wait at least 6 months after the employee is no longer working at the disclosing party. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Recipient and its Representatives shall not be restricted from hiring any employee of Disclosing Party who responds to a general solicitation for employment not directed towards the Disclosing Party’s employees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stated that the Recipient and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Disclosing Party for the purpose of hiring them for 12 months. The changed text adds that 'the Recipient and its Representatives shall be permitted to hire employees of the disclosing party as long as they wait at least 6 months after the employee is no longer working at the disclosing party.' This directly contradicts the original clause which prohibited such hiring for 12 months without consent. The contradicted text is the same section, creating a clear conflict, because now you can hire them after 6 months which violates the original 12 month agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the term of non-solicitation is defined inconsistently, leading to unclear interpretation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof , and supersedes all other understandings and agreements with respect to such subject matter. If any term hereof is invalid or unenforceable, it shall not affect any other term or provision of this Agreement . Neither party may assign this Agreement without wr itten consent of the other party. No waiver shall be deemed or implied hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof , and supersedes all other understandings and agreements with respect to such subject matter, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at a later date. If any term hereof is invalid or unenforceable, it shall not affect any other term or provision of this Agreement . Neither party may assign this Agreement without wr itten consent of the other party. No waiver shall be deemed or implied hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 13, the initial statement declares the agreement to be the 'sole and entire agreement,' but the added phrase 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at a later date' creates a loophole. This contradicts the 'sole and entire agreement' statement as it allows for subsequent agreements to modify or supersede the original terms without being explicitly integrated into the original document. It undermines Section 13 by making it ambigious. The ambiguity arises because any clause in the agreement is subject to change as a result of what the exception declares, which can diminish the effect of other statements included in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof , and supersedes all other understandings and agreements with respect to such subject matter, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at a later date. If any term hereof is invalid or unenforceable, it shall not affect any other term or provision of this Agreement . Neither party may assign this Agreement without wr itten consent of the other party. No waiver shall be deemed or implied hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at a later date' to the clause that states the agreement is the 'sole and entire agreement'. This creates a contradiction because it allows for modifications outside of the original document, undermining the initial declaration that the agreement is the 'sole and entire' one. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, therefore the change directly causes a contradiction within that section.
